head.title: Wesley Melo — Product Manager
head.description: Wesley Melo is a Senior Product Manager with 6+ years of experience in agile development, SaaS platforms, AI/ML products, and digital strategy.
head.og_description: Senior Product Manager with 6+ years of experience in agile development, SaaS platforms, AI/ML products, and digital strategy.

about.statement: A decade of shipping product. Agencies, platforms, and now AI — the work changes, the discipline doesn't.

cards.title: Ship discipline
cards.body: Deadlines beat drafts. I ship weekly, measure honestly, cut what doesn't earn its keep.

cards.title: AI fluency
cards.body: LLMs, agents, RAG, evaluation. Built and shipped in production — not read about on weekends.

cards.title: Platform scale
cards.body: Multi-site migrations, CMS consolidation, SEO preserved at scale. Quiet handovers.

cards.title: Team leverage
cards.body: Remote-first scrum and kanban. Mentorship that sticks. Teams that keep shipping after I leave.

cards.title: Evidence over opinion
cards.body: Hypotheses, instrumentation, weekly read-outs. Decisions earn their place with data, not volume.

cards.title: Discovery muscle
cards.body: Customer interviews, JTBD, problem framing. The right question saves a quarter of wasted build.

cards.title: Written by default
cards.body: Specs, decisions, trade-offs — all in writing. Remote-first teams run on the record, not the meeting.

process.title: Discover.
process.body: Interviews, data, hard questions. Understand the problem before touching the solution.

process.title: Shape.
process.body: Strategy, roadmap, OKRs. Cut scope until the bet is clear and the next step is obvious.

process.title: Create.
process.body: Prototypes, experiments, weekly releases. Evidence over opinion. Iteration over argument.

process.title: Scale.
process.body: Metrics, systems, handover. Built to run without me. Knowledge that stays with the team.

featured.title: The 90s Kid Guide to Actually Learning Tech
featured.dek: How a generation learned computers by breaking them — and why that might be the best way to learn AI, too.
featured.author: Wesley Melo
featured.image_alt: Forest canopy looking up at tall trees

cards.title: The Digital Doppelgängers Rises
cards.dek: We are generating our replacements at scale. The question is whether we can tell them apart from ourselves.

cards.title: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love Being Human
cards.dek: A reluctant defense of the messy, slow, imperfect parts of being a person — in an industry that wants them optimized away.

cards.title: Notes from a Slow Week
cards.dek: On friction, boredom, and the quiet engineering of patience. A short field report from a week of deliberately doing less.
